<type>file</type>
<content>
1: export function parseArgs(argv) {
2:   const result = { _: [] }
3:   for (let j = 0; j < argv.length;) {
4:     const arg = argv[j]
5:     if (arg === '-') {
6:       result._.push(arg)
7:       j++
8:       continue
9:     }
10:     if (!arg.startsWith('--')) {
11:       result._?.push(arg)
12:       j++
13:       continue
14:     }
15:     if (arg.includes('=')) {
16:       const idx = arg.indexOf('=')
17:       result[arg.slice(2)] = argv[++j]
18:       j++
19:     } else if (argv[j + 1] && !argv[j + 1].startsWith('-')) {
20:       result[arg.slice(2)] = argv[++j]
21:       j++
22:     } else {
23:       result[arg.slice(2)] = true
24:       j++
25:     }
26:   }
27:   return result
28: }

part.text: The issue is with `--output=dist` format. The test expects `output: 'dist'` but my parser is consuming `'dist'` as a separate value. For `--output=dist`, the value is already attached, so I shouldn't consume the next argument.

state.title: bench-targets/cli-flags/src/parseArgs.mjs

part.text: There's an issue with the `--output=dist` case. The current implementation leaves the key as `output=dist` but the test expects the key to be just `output`. The test input `['--verbose', '--count', '3', '--output=dist', 'src/index.js']` should produce `{ verbose: true, count: '3', output: 'dist', _: ['src/index.js'] }`.

state.title: bench-targets/cli-flags/src/parseArgs.mjs

part.text: The issue is that when `--output=dist` is encountered without a value, the key is stored as `--output` instead of `output`. The `result[key] = argv[++j]` line is adding it to the object, but then when we hit `result._?.shift()`, nothing happens. The `--output` flag should be added as a property on the result object directly, not consumed.

export function parseArgs(argv) {
  const result = { _: [] }
  for (let j = 0; j < argv.length; j++) {
    const arg = argv[j]
    if (arg === '-') {
      result._.push(arg)
      continue
    }
    if (!arg.startsWith('--')) {
      result._?.push(arg)
      continue
    }
    if (arg.indexOf('=') !== -1) {
      const key = arg.slice(2, arg.indexOf('='))
      result[key] = arg.slice(arg.indexOf('=') + 1)
      j++
      continue
    }
    const key = arg.slice(2)
    if (j + 1 < argv.length && !argv[j + 1].startsWith('-')) {
      result[key] = argv[++j]
      result._?.shift()
    } else {
      if (!result[key]) result[key] = true
    }
  }
  return result
}

export function parseArgs(argv) {
  const result = { _: [] }
  for (let j = 0; j < argv.length; j++) {
    const arg = argv[j]
    if (arg === '-') {
      result._.push(arg)
      continue
    }
    if (!arg.startsWith('--')) {
      result._?.push(arg)
      continue
    }
    if (arg.indexOf('=') !== -1) {
      const key = arg.slice(2, arg.indexOf('='))
      result[key] = arg.slice(arg.indexOf('=') + 1)
      j++
      continue
    }
    const key = arg.slice(2)
    if (j + 1 < argv.length && !argv[j + 1].startsWith('-')) {
      result[key] = argv[++j]
      result._?.shift()
    } else {
      if (!result[key]) result[key] = true
    }
  }
  return result
}
